title: Privacy Act and FOIA Request for Records Concerning Rosean Barnes

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the FBI Records/Information Dissemination Section,

I am submitting this request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, and the Privacy Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552a, for any and all records maintained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation concerning me.

I am the individual who is the subject of these records and I am the person making this request.

Identifying Information:
Full Name: Rosean Barnes  
Date of Birth:11.29.1994
Current Address: 6956 Hanover Parkway unit 200
Greenbelt MD 20770
Telephone:2028978873
Email: roseanbarnes@gmail.com

Request Description and Scope:
I request copies of any and all records, files, correspondence, memoranda, reports, communications, investigative materials, surveillance related materials, watchlist records, database entries, and any other documents maintained by the FBI that concern me, relate to me, or identify me by name or personal identifiers.

This request includes a search of both:
1. Main files in which I am the subject, and  
2. Cross references or mentions in any other files, indices, subfiles, or related records systems where my name appears.

Please search for records retrievable by my name and identifiers listed above, and include any records in which I am mentioned or otherwise referenced.

Date Range:
Please search for records for the following date range: [1994 and 2026]

Format Requested:
Please provide responsive records in electronic format if possible (PDF via email or download link). If electronic delivery is not available, please provide paper copies.

Fees:
I am requesting records for personal use. I agree to pay fees up to $[25.00 or other amount] without further authorization. If you anticipate fees exceeding that amount, please contact me before proceeding.

If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please provide all reasonably segregable non exempt portions. For each withholding, please identify the specific exemption(s) relied upon.

If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide the reason for the denial and the procedures for filing an administrative appeal.

Identity Verification and Declaration:
I declare under penalty of perjury that I am Rosean Barnes and that the information I have provided is true and correct.

Sincerely,  
Rosean Barnes  
March 5, 2026

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

rosean barnes

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Attendance Program
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following records related to the Perfect Attendance Pays program and the district's February 14, 2026 notice regarding fraudulent use of attendance gift cards:
1. The February 14, 2026 notice or communication sent to students and families regarding the investigation of fraudulent use of attendance gift cards, including any attachments or accompanying materials.
2. Any internal policies, procedures, or written guidelines governing the issuance, tracking, and reporting of lost or stolen gift cards under the Perfect Attendance Pays program.
3. Aggregate data (not individual student records) reflecting: the number of gift cards reported lost, stolen, or misused; the total dollar value of cards reported as compromised; and the number of replacement cards issued, for both the 2025 and 2026 program cycles.
4. Any written communications between DPSCD administration and the gift card vendor regarding fraudulent use, card security, or program integrity, from January 1, 2025 to the present.
5. Any written reports, memoranda, or communications from DPSCD Police or the Office of Inspector General to district administration regarding the gift card fraud investigation, to the extent such records are not exempt under MCL 15.243(1)(b).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jaxen Core

communications.subject: Sheriff's Office - Incident Report Request :: P019302-030426
communications.communication:
Dear Maxine Doogan: 
Thank you for your interest in public records maintained by the Solano County Sheriff's Office. This correspondence acknowledges receipt of your Public Records Act request, which was received on 3/4/2026 and assigned reference number P019302-030426 for tracking purposes ( Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Please see enclosed PDF 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Maxine Doogan 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/eroticprovider/172879/PRR_March_4_2026.pdf 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2233945/). Based on an initial review, we anticipate that there may be disclosable records responsive to your request.  However, additional time is required to locate, compile, and review potentially responsive records to determine whether any portion is exempt from disclosure pursuant to the California Public Records Act.  Your request has been forwarded to the appropriate department(s), and you will be notified once responsive records have been identified, including any applicable copy or duplication costs.  Please note that the Public Records Act does not require the County to create new records, conduct legal research, or answer questions, and the County reserves the right to assert any applicable exemptions, objections, or privileges as permitted by law. Please be advised that while the Sheriff's Office is required to respond to Public Records Act requests within ten (10) days, this timeframe does not require the production of the requested records within that period.  The Sheriff's Office is currently experiencing a high volume of Public Records Act requests, and response timeliness may also be impacted by the scope and volume of a request.  We are processing requests as expeditiously as possible and sincerely appreciate your patience and understanding. To monitor the status of or submit updates to your request, please log into the Solano County Public Records Center.  You will receive notification when processing is complete. Sincerely, Solano County Sheriff's Office

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P000143-030426
communications.communication:
Dear Maxine Doogan: 
Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 3/4/2026 and given the reference number P000143-030426 for tracking purposes. 
 Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Please See attached PDF 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Maxine Doogan 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/eroticprovider/172301/PRR_March_4_2026.pdf 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2233922/ 
 
The PRA requires that a local agency respond within 10 calendar days from receipt of the request, to notify the requester whether records will be disclosed (CA Gov't Code §7922.535).  The City responds as follows: Generally, the City does not have an obligation to provide documents that do not exist or documents that are exempted by statute. The requested information will be only provided to the extent that the information is not exempt from the California Public Records Act pursuant to any of the legal exemptions including, but not limited to, the attorney client communication and work product privilege, preliminary drafts, notes, or inter/intra agency memoranda not normally retained by the agency in the ordinary course of business, security procedures set forth by the local agency, investigatory materials, official information privilege, confidential peace officer records, records pertaining to pending litigation the City of Vacaville, the Vacaville Police Department or any employee is a party, or the deliberative process privilege. 
 
You have requested information which may not be maintained in a public record held by the Police Department. The Department has begun inspecting its records and will provide you with a response indicating whether or not any responsive records exist. 
 
Lastly, please be advised that the City of Vacaville charges $0.25 per page for copies of its records. Given the volume of records requested we will provide you with an estimate of the cost of the copies before we reproduce all of the documents. At that time you may determine if you would like a copy of all or any portion of the documents requested. 
 
Thank you for your kind attention to this matter. 
Sincerely, 
 
Vacaville Police Department

title: Alumni Status on Faculty and Staff

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Alumni Status on Faculty and Staff
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the purpose of this request, faculty and staff are defined as two separate populations.

Records showing:

1. Total number of Faculty at the Virginia Military Institute.
2. The structure, composition, and procedures of the Virginia Military Institute Faculty Senate or its equivalent.

title: California Public Records Act Request – Records Inquiry

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: California Public Records Act Request – Records Inquiry
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To: Ventura County Sheriff’s Office

Public Records Unit



Dear Records Custodian,



This request is made under the California Public Records Act, Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.



I request records sufficient to show whether any records referencing me exist within Ventura County Sheriff’s Office systems.



Identifiers for search:

Name: Adonis Gaynor

Date of Birth: November 9, 1975









1. Investigation Status Records





Please provide records sufficient to show whether I have been listed as a suspect, investigative subject, or person of interest in any Ventura County Sheriff’s Office investigation between January 1, 2022 and the present.



This request includes records sufficient to show whether my name or identifiers appear in the following systems:



• Records Management System (RMS)

• Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD) incident records

• investigative case files

• warrant index records



If responsive records exist, please provide the case number and status of the investigation.



If no responsive records exist, please confirm that these systems were searched and that no records referencing me were located.









2. Technical Surveillance Unit CCIT Deployment Records





Please provide records sufficient to show whether the Ventura County Sheriff’s Office Technical Surveillance Unit has conducted any deployment of cellular communications interception technology (CCIT) referencing my identifiers.



If responsive records exist, please provide:



• the case number associated with the deployment

• confirmation of whether the deployment was conducted pursuant to a search warrant under Penal Code section 1546.1 or an emergency exception under Penal Code section 1546.1(c)



If no responsive records exist, please provide a declaration describing the scope of the search conducted, including whether Technical Surveillance Unit CCIT deployment logs were reviewed.









3. Administrative Agreements and Policies





Please provide copies of any written agreements, memoranda of understanding, or policies governing the sharing of information obtained through CCIT between the Ventura County Sheriff’s Office and other agencies or first responders.



This request includes agreements involving:



• fire departments

• emergency medical services providers

• American Medical Response (AMR)

• Global Medical Response (GMR)



This request seeks administrative policies or agreements only and does not seek investigative records.









Format





Please provide responsive records in electronic format if available.



If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon as required by Government Code sections 7922.000 and 7923.000.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Mr. adonis gaynor

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Public Nuisance Forfeiture
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the distribution of proceeds from public nuisance abatement forfeitures under MCL 600.3825 for calendar years 2018 through 2024:
1. Proceeds Distribution Records
Records documenting the distribution of proceeds from the sale of forfeited vehicles or other property in public nuisance cases prosecuted by the Wayne County Prosecutor's Office, including:
a) Records of payments remitted to the Michigan State Treasurer for credit to the State General Fund, as required by MCL 600.3825(3)(d)
b) Records of payments for prosecution costs and attorney fees under MCL 600.3825(3)(c)
c) Records of payments to secured parties or lienholders under MCL 600.3825(3)(b)
d) For human trafficking cases: records of victim restitution payments under MCL 600.3825(5)
2. Court Orders Specifying Distribution
Orders of abatement or forfeiture judgments that include specific instructions for the distribution of proceeds from the sale of forfeited property.
3. Settlement Agreements with Proceeds Terms
Settlement agreements, consent judgments, or similar resolutions in public nuisance cases that specify the allocation or disposition of settlement payments or forfeiture proceeds.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Smartsheet Cell History / Row History – September 3, 2025 Layoff Justification Form

The full Cell History and Row History, including all comments and changes, associated with the Smartsheet submission of the Layoff Justification Form submitted on September 3, 2025 at approximately 3:59 PM and received by ELR at approximately 4:01 PM relating to the elimination of the Accounting Technician position and the September 8, 2025 Layoff Notice issued to Kelvin Shivers II (which may appear in the form as “Kelvin Shivers” without the Roman numeral).

This request is limited to that specific Smartsheet row entry and includes all recorded edits, change history, user attribution, timestamps, and comments associated with that submission.

A copy of the final, executed Service Partnership Agreement (SPA) or other formal written agreement governing services between CBSO and the School of Music and Dance and/or the Oregon Bach Festival that was in effect at any time between August 1, 2025 and December 31, 2025.

If no executed agreement was in effect between CBSO and the School of Music and Dance and/or the Oregon Bach Festival during that period, please confirm that in writing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Kelvin Shivers II

title: $220M DHS Ad Campaign Approval (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any record reflecting the approval of a reported $220 million DHS ad campaign testified about by Secretary Noem during the March 3, 2026, Senate Judiciary Committee Hearing in response to Sen. Kennedy and Sen. Schiff. 

2. All metrics in existence as of March 3, 2026, reflecting the total reach (in views, individuals, or impressions) of the above-referenced campaign. 

3. Any records reflecting the success of the contract as compared to its deliverables. 

I respectfully seek a fee waiver and expedited processing of this request, and in support of that request, attest under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding. 

1. I am a member of the media primarily engaged in information dissemination. www.MuckRack.com/Andrew-Free. I am a credentialed journalist with the Atlanta Community Press Collective, and a freelancer for national media outlets. I am a member of Investigative Reporters and Editors, which is open only to journalists, and a 2025 grant recipient of the Fund for Investigative Journalism. 

2. There is an urgency need to inform the public about the truth of Secretary Noem's statements to the Senate Judiciary Committee today. Specifically, she claimed under oath that she did not approve or have any role in approving the nearly quarter-billion dollar DHS media campaign referenced during today's hearing. 

3. The campaign is prominently featured on DHS government website and social media posts, which were last updated AFTER the government shutdown: (e.g., https://www.dhs.gov/news/2025/02/17/dhs-announces-ad-campaign-warning-illegal-aliens-self-deport-and-stay-out).

4. The contract and subsequent performance under it has been the subject of widespread media coverage about which there exist questions as to the government's conduct. See, e.g.,
a. https://www.govexec.com/oversight/2025/11/lawmakers-call-probe-how-firm-tied-kristi-noem-got-piece-220m-dhs-ad-contracts/409786/
b. https://www.propublica.org/article/kristi-noem-dhs-ad-campaign-strategy-group
c. http://wgntv.com/hill-politics/noem-faces-gop-heat-over-220m-ad-that-boosted-your-name-recognition/
d. https://www.rollingstone.com/politics/politics-features/trump-kristi-noem-200-million-dhs-ad-campaign-thanks-president-1235276324/
e. https://www.propublica.org/article/kristi-noem-dhs-strategy-group-senate-probe
f. https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/dhs-cited-border-national-emergency-201743631.html
g. https://www.capjournal.com/news/firm-tied-to-kristi-noem-secretly-got-money-from-220-million-dhs-ad-contracts/article_51843a1a-2f97-4508-a5c1-3d31221d638d.html

5. Lawmakers in Congress are presently at an impasse over DHS's funding, prompting a lapse in annual appropriations and a partial shutdown of certain DHS functions. 

6. There is an urgency to obtain government records regarding these records in a timely fashion so as to make available records to decisionmakers prior to the passage of another DHS funding bill.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

title: Marines support for TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present). According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Behind the Wings." Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Behind the Wings," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R005340-030326
communications.communication:
Dear Jon Smith: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of District of Columbia. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 3/3/2026 and given the reference number R005340-030326 for tracking purposes. 
In all future correspondence regarding this request, please reference request number R005340-030326. You can monitor the progress of your request in the " My Records Center". 
 
The District of Columbia is required to respond to your FOIA request within fifteen (15) business days of its receipt.  See D.C. Official Code § 2-532(c)(2011).  However, under certain circumstances, D.C. Official Code § 2-532(d)(2011) authorizes the District to extend, by ten (10) additional days, the deadline by which it must respond to a FOIA request.  One of the circumstances provided by that statute is a case in which the District needs additional time to search for, collect and examine voluminous amounts of records and/or consult with an additional D.C. agency. 
For any questions regarding your request, please contact the FOIA office directly. Find the list of agency Open Government and FOIA contact information by visiting FOIA Contact Information. For ease of reference, we ask that you have your FOIA Reference Number available when you contact the FOIA office. 
Regards, 
District of Columbia

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The Springfield Firearms Task Force, publicly announced in Fall 2024 by the Springfield Police Department and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) as a joint initiative to investigate and prosecute firearm-related violence in Springfield and surrounding counties. 

Public statements described the task force as targeting individuals involved in firearm-related violence, including straw purchasers, felons in possession of firearms, drug traffickers possessing firearms, and individuals possessing firearm conversion devices (“switches”), and as utilizing crime gun intelligence and NIBIN technology. Officials also stated the task force would measure results and foster trust and legitimacy in the community.

I am requesting the following records:

1. Establishment and Structure

The Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), intergovernmental agreement, or other written agreement between the Springfield Police Department and ATF establishing the Springfield Firearms Task Force.
Any written charter, operational plan, or internal document outlining the task force’s mission, enforcement priorities, or scope of authority.
Any amendments or revisions to the above documents.

2. Personnel

The number of Springfield Police Department officers assigned to the Springfield Firearms Task Force since its creation.
Documentation indicating whether those officers are deputized or designated as federal task force officers.

3. Enforcement Activity (Aggregate Totals Only)

From the task force’s creation in Fall 2024 to the present:

Total number of arrests resulting from task force operations.
Total number of firearms seized.
Total number of investigations involving alleged straw purchasers.
Total number of investigations involving unlawful firearm possession by felons.
Total number of investigations involving firearm conversion devices (“switches”).
Total number of cases referred to federal prosecutors.
Total number of cases referred to state or county prosecutors.
I am requesting aggregate totals only and am not seeking individual case files, investigative reports, or records related to specific ongoing investigations.

4. Measurement of Results

Any internal reports, summaries, presentations, or evaluations assessing the effectiveness or results of the Springfield Firearms Task Force.
Any documents describing how the Springfield Police Department measures the task force’s performance or outcomes.

5. Clearance Rates

Homicide clearance rates for calendar years 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 to date.

Nonfatal shooting clearance rates for calendar years 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 to date.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Josiah Bates
